statement.p: The population of deer in a forest can be modeled by
where x is the number of years since the year 1900.

li.p: How many deer lived in this forest in the year 1900?
How many deer live in this forest 29 years after 1900?
How many years since 1900 was it (or will it be) when the deer population was (or will be) 393?
Use a calculator or graphing calculator to answer this question: As time goes on, the population levels off at about how many deer?
How many deer live in this forest this year? To answer this question, we substitute x with 0, and we have:
About 230 deer live in this forest this year.
How many deer will live in this forest 29 years later? To answer this question, we substitute x with 29, and we have:
About 397 deer will live in this forest 29 years later.
After how many years, the deer population will be 393? To answer this question, we substitute P(x) with 393, and then solve for x:
After about 13 years, the deer population will be 393.

Use a graphing calculator to answer this question: Very many years in the future, about how many deer will live in this forest? To answer this question, we substitute x with a very big number, like 10{,}000, and we have:
Many years in the future, about 400 deer will live in this forest.
